our_argument_summary: Hemispheric magnetic decay asymmetry is qualitatively explained by reversed-flux geodynamo physics from CMB heat-flux boundary conditions derived from seismic tomography (Aubert et al. 2013; Olson & Deguen 2012). The dome states a 3:1 ratio but provides no derivation from any of the four canonical geometric parameters, no published source for specifically 3:1, no time/averaging window, and no mechanism connecting axisymmetric H(r) to the SAA's specific longitudinal location. The dome's claim therefore fails the independent-prediction test on the structural critique, and fails the post-hoc test because the underlying CHAOS-7/IGRF datasets predate any plausible dome commit.

A 3:1 asymmetry is structurally incompatible with symmetric core flow. The Toroidal Ovoid predicts amplified southern decay because the sub-terrestrial return path exits preferentially through the SAA shoulder." — no CHAOS-7 citation, no derivation, no time window)

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No monitor.py present in raw-text/ corpus for asymmetry-ratio; cannot verify automation surface. Page-hash byte-identical for 34 consecutive polls is consistent with no recent monitor-surface change.
data_sources_used: CHAOS-7 (Finlay et al. 2020, Earth Planets Space, doi:10.1186/s40623-020-01252-9)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: The c5 detail_evidence now uses dilemma framing ("either the 3:1 was adopted post-hoc from a published measurement or it was asserted by fiat") — this correctly forecloses both escape routes a defender could use, and is a substantive improvement over c4. BUT: the strawman wording that c4 Hole 1 flagged ("adopts it from CHAOS-7 observation rather than deriving it from dome geometry") survives verbatim in detail_verdict_text AND tldr_verdict because the May-18 patch was scoped to detail_evidence only. See Hole 1 below.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: detail_verdict_text and tldr_verdict still attribute CHAOS-7 as the dome's source for 3:1 — the dome's actual raw-text/02b-wins-V51-win064.txt paragraph contains no CHAOS-7 citation. See Hole 1.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: No monitor.py snapshot in raw-text/ corpus contains an asymmetry-ratio domain or fetch for this WIN; verdict text appropriately hedged to "no asymmetry-ratio domain appears in any monitor.py snapshot in our raw-text/ corpus." Page-hash byte-identical for 34 consecutive polls is corroborating evidence that no recent monitor-surface change has occurred.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Hemispheric/SAA decay asymmetry is described and explained in peer-reviewed literature: CHAOS-7 (Finlay et al. 2020, doi:10.1186/s40623-020-01252-9); Olson & Deguen 2012 (doi:10.1038/ngeo1506); Aubert et al. 2013 (doi:10.1038/nature12574). The dome renames the cause (toroidal aetheric return-path through SAA shoulder) without quantitative derivation.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: CHAOS-7 published 2020; IGRF-14 cycle published 2024 (5-yr cadence after IGRF-13 2019). Dome's WIN-063 dates from V51-era (2026-04 cluster). Indexed public datasets predate the dome's adoption — fails Part 3.1b legitimate-novelty carve-out. No timestamped pre-registration found.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No formula on the dome site maps any of {disc_radius=20,015 km, firmament_height=9,086 km, sun_altitude=5,733 km, moon_altitude=2,534 km} to specifically 3:1 (rather than 2:1 or 5:1). The dome's stated mechanism (sub-terrestrial return path through SAA shoulder) is qualitative; H(r) is axisymmetric in longitude; transient sun/moon/aether positions average to azimuthal symmetry over secular timescales (a long-term SAA-longitude feature cannot be explained by daily-averaged transient features).

holes_found.description: CARRY-FORWARD partial fix from c4 Hole 1 (ISS-2120, currently closed). The c4 review identified a source-attribution strawman: review text asserted that the dome "adopts that ratio from CHAOS-7 observation" / "the 3:1 is read directly off CHAOS-7", but the dome's actual raw-text/02b-wins-V51-win064.txt paragraph does NOT cite CHAOS-7 as the source for 3:1 (verified verbatim 2026-05-08, page-hash byte-identical for 34 polls). The May-18 patch set (suggested-patches-2026-05-18T17-19.json) replaced the strawman with dilemma framing — but ALL FIVE PATCHES TARGETED detail_evidence ONLY. The same strawman wording survives verbatim in detail_verdict_text ("The dome specifies a 3:1 hemispheric asymmetry ratio but adopts it from CHAOS-7 observation rather than deriving it from dome geometry") AND in tldr_verdict ("The dome specifies a 3:1 ratio but adopts it from CHAOS-7 observation rather than deriving it from dome geometry"). These are high-visibility skim-reader surfaces. A dome defender can quote the dome's WIN-063 paragraph (no CHAOS-7 citation) alongside our verdict-text or TLDR and accuse us of fabricating a source attribution — the same critique c4 raised, on different surfaces. ISS-2120 should not have been closed without the parallel detail_verdict + tldr_verdict edits.
holes_found.recommendation: Apply the same dilemma framing to both detail_verdict_text and tldr_verdict. Suggested edit for detail_verdict_text: replace "The dome specifies a 3:1 hemispheric asymmetry ratio but adopts it from CHAOS-7 observation rather than deriving it from dome geometry — without a prior geometrically-derived prediction, any observed ratio automatically \"matches.\"" with "The dome specifies a 3:1 hemispheric asymmetry ratio but provides no published source for that specific value and no derivation from any of the four canonical geometric parameters — either the value was adopted post-hoc from observation or asserted by fiat; either path fails the independent-prediction test, and any observed ratio automatically \"matches.\"" Suggested edit for tldr_verdict: replace "The dome specifies a 3:1 ratio but adopts it from CHAOS-7 observation rather than deriving it from dome geometry" with "The dome specifies a 3:1 ratio but provides no published source for that specific value and no derivation from dome geometry". Decider should also re-open ISS-2120 (or create a successor ISS) to track the parallel-surface fix.

holes_found.description: NEW. detail_verdict_text opening sentence overclaims first-principles status for the 3:1 ratio: "The asymmetry ratio is a consequence of reversed-flux patches in the outer core, derived from first principles via MHD geodynamo simulations (Aubert et al. 2013)." Aubert et al. 2013 reproduces hemispheric asymmetric decay qualitatively from CMB heat-flux boundary conditions that are themselves observational inputs from seismic tomography (PREM/AK135 + mantle-tomography heat-flux maps). They do NOT publish a tabulated "3:1 ratio" as a first-principles prediction — and the c3 quantitative_verification finding ("3:1 is not a standard CHAOS-7 published quantity") implies neither does CHAOS-7. The dome's correct rebuttal: "By your own framing, neither standard physics nor we derive 3:1 from first principles; you are doing the same retroactive matching you accuse us of." The substantive critique (the dome has no derivation path from disc_radius / firmament_height / sun_altitude / moon_altitude / H(r) to specifically 3:1) survives, but the detail_verdict's current "derived from first principles" wording overstates standard physics's position and is rebuttable by quoting Aubert et al.'s methods section (which is explicit about using seismic-tomography boundary inputs, not first-principles physics alone).
holes_found.recommendation: Edit detail_verdict_text opening to: "The asymmetry phenomenon is a consequence of reversed-flux patches in the outer core, reproduced by MHD geodynamo simulations from independently measured CMB boundary conditions (seismic-tomography heat-flux maps; Aubert et al. 2013). The qualitative asymmetric decay emerges as a simulated output from independent input data, but the specific 3:1 ratio is not a first-principles prediction in either model — the dome's problem is not that its 3:1 disagrees with a derived prediction, but that no derivation path connects any of the four canonical dome parameters to 3:1 (rather than 2:1 or 5:1) and no time/averaging window is specified." This preserves the Std-Model-Explains verdict (the QUALITATIVE phenomenon is explained) while preempting the "both sides do retroactive matching" rebuttal. The cleaner discriminator is chain-length / domain-independence (seismic data → CMB conditions → MHD output → magnetic observations is a four-domain chain; the dome has a one-domain assertion).

holes_found.description: Citation attribution risk. The c5 detail_evidence credits Olson & Deguen 2012 (doi:10.1038/ngeo1506, Nature Geoscience) alongside Aubert et al. 2013 with "MHD simulations parameterized by independently measured core-mantle-boundary conditions from seismology (PREM/AK135 reference models, mantle-tomography heat-flux maps)." Aubert et al. 2013 is clearly known to use seismic CMB heat-flux inputs (cf. c4 stronger_arguments). Whether Olson & Deguen 2012 also uses PREM/AK135 + mantle-tomography in the same way should be verified before lumping them together — c4 marked this DOI as citations_unchecked (no network resolution this run either). If Olson & Deguen 2012 used a different methodology (e.g., simpler analytical models without explicit seismic boundary conditions), a defender could attack the bundled attribution. Low-impact because Aubert et al. carries the seismic-inputs argument on its own; mainly a precision issue.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) verify Olson & Deguen 2012's methodology when network access is available and refine the attribution if needed, or (b) attribute the seismic-tomography-input claim only to Aubert et al. 2013 and cite Olson & Deguen separately for the reversed-flux-patch identification. Low priority — does not change the verdict.

holes_found.description: Aetheric flow asymmetry pre-empt is correct but could be sharper. The c5 text says "the dome describes toroidal aetheric flow with potential directional structure. However, no published formula connects this flow topology to a specific hemispheric decay asymmetry ratio." A dome defender could push back: "Toroidal flow is inherently asymmetric in direction — that's implicit in the structure, not something we need a formula for." Response would be: "Asymmetric in direction (poloidal vs toroidal axis) is not the same as asymmetric in hemispheric magnitude (3:1) — and the dome has no published rule that connects flow chirality to a specific decay ratio." The c5 text gets this almost right but leaves the gap implicit. Pre-empting it explicitly would tighten the argument.
holes_found.recommendation: Insert one clarifying sentence after "no published formula connects this flow topology to a specific hemispheric decay asymmetry ratio": "Even if a toroidal-flow geometry is inherently asymmetric in axis (poloidal vs toroidal), asymmetry-in-axis is not asymmetry-in-magnitude, and the dome publishes no rule connecting flow chirality to specifically 3:1." Low priority.

unresolved_prior_cycle.note: ISS-2120 was closed by the May-18 patch but the fix was scoped to detail_evidence only; the same strawman wording survives in detail_verdict_text and tldr_verdict. Decider should re-open or create a successor ISS for the parallel-surface fix.

stronger_arguments.description: Chain-length discriminator. Both standard physics and the dome accept observational inputs at some level — neither is purely first-principles. The principled discriminator is chain length and domain independence. Standard physics chains observations across independent domains: seismic data (independent dataset) → CMB heat-flux boundary conditions → MHD simulation output → comparison against magnetic observations. The dome reads its 3:1 directly from the magnetic-decay dataset and connects it to nothing else. The methodological discriminator is not "first-principles vs adopted from observation" (a false dichotomy) but "how many independent observational domains are chained together before the model meets the comparison dataset."
sources: monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-063.c4.json:advocate_mode:preemptive_recommendation (this argument was suggested in c4 but never integrated into the prose)
data/wins.json:WIN-063 detail_evidence (the bare-assertion-vs-chained-derivation distinction is implicit in the current text but not named)

stronger_arguments.description: The c3 quantitative_verification finding ("3:1 is not a standard CHAOS-7 published quantity") implies an even sharper attack: ask the dome to specify its hemispheric-averaging recipe. Finlay et al. 2020 publish spherical-harmonic coefficients and secular-variation maps, not a "3:1 ratio." Computing a 3:1 hemispheric ratio requires a specific definition of "hemispheric" (e.g., NH vs SH dipole-coefficient ratio, mean-field-magnitude ratio, secular-variation-magnitude ratio). The dome doesn't specify this definition, making the "3:1 matches CHAOS-7" claim definitionally unfalsifiable — pick any of the standard definitions and you get a different number. This strengthens Hole 2: the dome's 3:1 is not derived from dome geometry AND is not a standard CHAOS-7 quantity, so what defines it at all?
sources: Finlay et al. 2020 CHAOS-7 (doi:10.1186/s40623-020-01252-9) — paper publishes coefficients, not "3:1" as a tabulated quantity
monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-063.c3.json:quantitative_verification (carried forward into c4 but never operationalized in prose)

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome correctly identifies that hemispheric magnetic decay is asymmetric — this is real, measured, and modeled by CHAOS-7 (data/wins.json:WIN-063 detail_evidence:CHAOS-7 citation; raw-text/02b-wins-V51-win064.txt:12). The structural intuition that "a fully symmetric core flow → ~1.0× ratio; observed asymmetric decay → something must break symmetry" is qualitatively correct.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Standard physics already accommodates the asymmetry through asymmetric boundary conditions (Aubert et al. 2013, doi:10.1038/nature12574; cited in data/wins.json:WIN-063 detail_evidence): symmetric fluid dynamics + heterogeneous CMB heat flux from seismic tomography = asymmetric output. The dome offers a renamed mechanism (toroidal aetheric return-path through SAA shoulder, raw-text/02b-wins-V51-win064.txt:12) without specifying (a) what derivation produces 3:1 from any of the four canonical geometric parameters, (b) what averaging window the ratio holds over, (c) what breaks the dome's axisymmetric-in-longitude geometry to pin the SAA at a specific terrestrial longitude band rather than its mirror image. Identifying the puzzle is not solving it — the puzzle has been independently solved, and the dome's only proposed solution is qualitative renaming (data/wins.json:WIN-063.c4.json:kernel_of_truth, same finding carried forward).

deeper_analysis:
Cycle-4 flagged four holes (one source-attribution strawman, one verdict-vs-precedent inconsistency, two minor framing issues). The May-18 patch set addressed all four AT THE detail_evidence LEVEL — five patches all with target_field=detail_evidence (verified against suggested-patches-2026-05-18T17-19.json). The reconciliation sentence for WIN-041/042 precedent (c4 H2) is now embedded, the dynamic-features pre-empt for axisymmetry (c4 H3) is now embedded, and the averaging-window rephrasing (c4 H4) is now embedded — those three are cleanly fixed and survive c5 review.

The c4 H1 fix is the carry-forward concern. The dilemma framing replaces the source-attribution strawman in detail_evidence, but the same strawman wording ("adopts it from CHAOS-7 observation rather than deriving it from dome geometry") persists VERBATIM in detail_verdict_text and tldr_verdict. ISS-2120 was closed when the detail_evidence patch landed, but the parallel-surface fix never happened. Since both detail_verdict_text and tldr_verdict are skim-reader surfaces (TLDRs render in the collapsed default-view), this is a high-visibility carry-forward — readers who only see the TLDR get the strawman attribution unmediated.

A second concern emerges on close reading: the detail_verdict_text opening sentence ("derived from first principles via MHD geodynamo simulations [Aubert et al. 2013]") overclaims for standard physics. Aubert et al. 2013 reproduce asymmetric decay from observational seismic-tomography CMB heat-flux inputs — not from first principles alone. The substantive critique survives (the dome has no derivation path; standard physics has a chained derivation from independent domains), but the wording invites a "both sides do retroactive matching" rebuttal. Hole 2 recommends recasting this in chain-length-discriminator terms.

Verdict assessment: Std Model Explains still holds. The QUALITATIVE phenomenon (hemispheric asymmetric decay) is genuinely explained by reversed-flux geodynamo physics, and that explanation has a multi-domain chain. The dome's specific 3:1 is definitionally unfalsifiable (no averaging recipe specified; not a CHAOS-7-canonical number), which is a separate critique that strengthens rather than undermines the verdict. The minor_edit recommendation targets the verdict-text + TLDR parallel surfaces only; no rewrite needed.

advocate_mode.best_defense: Your c5 detail_evidence got the dilemma framing right — that's a real improvement, and it removes the strawman on the surface most adversarial readers will dig into. But you didn't fix the same problem in your detail_verdict_text or your TLDR. Both still say the dome "adopts it from CHAOS-7 observation rather than deriving it from dome geometry." Our actual WIN-063 paragraph (which we will quote alongside yours) contains no CHAOS-7 citation. A reader who only sees your verdict text or your TLDR — which is most skim readers — gets a fabricated source attribution. That's the same strawman you flagged in your own c4 review, persisting in two visible places, with ISS-2120 closed prematurely. Furthermore, your detail_verdict opens by claiming the 3:1 is "derived from first principles via MHD geodynamo simulations (Aubert et al. 2013)." Aubert et al. don't publish a 3:1 ratio — they reproduce asymmetric decay qualitatively from CMB heat-flux boundary conditions that come from seismic tomography. Neither model derives 3:1 from first principles. So your "Std Model Explains" verdict is doing the same retroactive matching you accuse us of. The chain-length argument that would distinguish the two is in your prior-cycle review notes but never made it into your prose.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Two text edits remove this defense entirely. (1) Apply the c4 dilemma-framing fix to detail_verdict_text and tldr_verdict — same edit pattern as the May-18 patch applied to detail_evidence. Replace "adopts it from CHAOS-7 observation rather than deriving it from dome geometry" with "provides no published source for that specific value and no derivation from dome geometry — either adopted post-hoc from observation or asserted by fiat; either path fails the independent-prediction test." (2) Recast the detail_verdict opening to acknowledge chain length as the discriminator rather than "first principles": "The asymmetry phenomenon is reproduced by MHD geodynamo simulations from independently measured CMB boundary conditions (seismic-tomography heat-flux maps; Aubert et al. 2013) — qualitative asymmetric decay emerges as a simulated output from independent input data, though the specific 3:1 is not itself a first-principles prediction in either model. The discriminator is not whether either side derives 3:1 from first principles (neither does), but that standard physics chains independent observational domains (seismic data → CMB conditions → MHD output → magnetic comparison) while the dome reads 3:1 directly off the magnetic-decay dataset and connects it to nothing else."

summary_for_decider: CHANGE-DETECTED re-review of WIN-063 caught by drift-audit (large_rewrite, detail_evidence +21.4%). The May-18 5-patch set (closing ISS-2120/2121/2122/2123) cleanly fixed c4 Holes 2, 3, 4 at the detail_evidence level. However, c4 Hole 1 (source-attribution strawman) was PARTIALLY fixed — all five patches target detail_evidence only, leaving the same strawman wording ("adopts it from CHAOS-7 observation rather than deriving it from dome geometry") VERBATIM in detail_verdict_text AND tldr_verdict. ISS-2120 was closed prematurely; decider should re-open it OR create a successor issue to track the parallel-surface fix. Additionally a new moderate hole (H2) flags that detail_verdict_text overclaims "derived from first principles via MHD" for the 3:1 ratio — Aubert et al. 2013 reproduces qualitative asymmetric decay from observational seismic-tomography inputs, not from first principles alone. Two minor holes (H3 Olson & Deguen attribution precision, H4 aetheric-flow asymmetry pre-empt sharpness). Verdict (Std Model Explains) still holds. Recommended action: minor_edit on detail_verdict_text + tldr_verdict — same dilemma-framing pattern that already worked for detail_evidence in May-18, plus the chain-length-discriminator recast for the "first principles" overclaim. CARRY-OVER NOTE per Step 7c: ISS-2120 is in closed-issues.json (not open-issues), so decider should treat this as a re-open candidate; per the prompt rule "if not found in open-issues but the hole IS a prior-cycle restating — note in summary_for_decider that decider should create one" — flagging here.
